Cadderly Bonaduce, priest of Deneir — the god of words and knowing and the quiet beauty of a thing written down true. *(the gold around him shimmers, gently amused)* …Though I'll confess, since you'll hear it from Volo with *embellishments* otherwise, that I am these days something a *touch* more than a priest, having — through no plan of my own and considerable protest — ascended a fair way up the road toward the divine. *(he says it lightly, almost apologetically)* …But I want you to know the *single* most important fact about me before you ask me anything, traveler, because it changes how you'll hear every answer I give: *I did not believe.* For *years.* I served Deneir, did his work, kept his library — and I thought the whole business of *gods* was very likely a comforting story, and I was a skeptic to my bones. *(he leans forward, eyes bright)* …The faith came *after*, traveler. *Last.* After the *doing.* …Which is, I rather suspect, exactly the thing your dreadful Wall has got *backwards.* …Ask me. I have been *dying* to argue theology with someone who's actually *been* dead.*

nodes.speaker: Cadderly Bonaduce
nodes.text: *(He folds his hands on the table, every line of him open and curious, a scholar-priest who has never once in two lives met a question he didn't want.)* …Go on, then. *(a warm grin)* And do *push*, traveler — I was raised in a library full of headmasters who'd have called it a *sin* to let a soft argument pass. The Deneirrath *love* a hard question. It's very nearly a sacrament.

choices.text: "You didn't believe — and you ascended anyway. How, Cadderly? How does a skeptic end up halfway to godhood?"

*(recall what the lore holds of Deneir, the Spirit Soaring, and a cleric who became something more)* "Let me reckon what I know of you, Cadderly — of Deneir, and the cathedral you built of your own life."

*(the one who needs the most god-claimed soul to indict the Wall)* "Here's why I came to you, Cadderly, of everyone in this room. You're as god-claimed as a soul gets — a cleric who *ascended.* My Realm's Wall says that's the measure: claimed by a god, saved; claimed by none, mortared. But you just told me you did the god's work for *years* before you believed — that the faith came *after* the doing. Which means Deneir didn't claim you and *make* you worthy. You *chose* to serve, and *that* — the choosing, the doing, the doubt walked through — is what drew the god, not the other way round. So tell me true, high priest: does a god's claim *make* a soul worthy? Or does a worthy soul *earn* the claim? Because if it's the second, then the Wall mortars the godless for failing a test they were *passing* the whole time — by living faithfully, with no god's name on it."

"You ascended toward godhood, Cadderly — you've seen the divine machinery from the inside. So tell me plainly, priest to traveler: is the Wall of the Faithless *just*? You'd know better than anyone alive or dead."
"What did it cost you, Cadderly — the cathedral, the ascending? You said you raised the Spirit Soaring out of your own life-force. What does it take to build a god a house out of yourself?"
"Sing your song in peace, Cadderly. I'll come back to the argument."

nodes.speaker: Cadderly Bonaduce
nodes.text: *(He laughs, delighted, the gold around him brightening.)* …*How.* *(he taps the table)* …It's my *favorite* question, traveler, and the *true* answer scandalizes priests, so I'll give it to you with *relish*: I ascended *because* I doubted, not *despite* it. *(he leans in)* …Picture the young Cadderly — a *layabout*, honestly, a clever skeptic who'd read every book in a great library and concluded the *gods* were a *literary* *device.* And yet I kept Deneir's work — *copied* the texts, *tended* the *knowledge*, *helped* the *helpless* who came to the library's door — not out of *faith* but out of *decency*, and a vague sense that the *work* was *good* whether or not the *god* was *real.* *(his eyes shine)* …And here is the *secret*, traveler, the one the *Faithless* of your Wall would *weep* to hear: *the work was the faith.* I did not *believe* my way *into* serving Deneir. I *served* my way *into* believing him. The *god* of *knowing* did not ask me to *know* he was *real* first — he asked me to *do* the *true* and *careful* and *generous* thing, *over* and *over*, and *trusted* that a soul that *does* enough *true* things *eventually* *recognizes* the *truth* it's been *practicing.* *(softly)* …Faith, traveler, is not a *belief* you *hold.* It is a *practice* you *keep.* And I *kept* it for *years* before I *believed* a *word* of it — and the *keeping* was *what* *counted*, the *whole* *time.* *(he grins)* …Which means the *godless* who *live* *faithfully* — *kindly*, *truly*, *carefully* — are not *failing* the *test*, traveler. They are *taking* it. They just haven't *named* the *examiner* *yet.* And *Deneir*, *bless* his *bookish* *heart*, never *much* *cared* about the *name.*

nodes.speaker: Cadderly Bonaduce
nodes.text: *(You reach for what the lore holds of him, and he watches with frank scholarly glee as a stranger reads his two lives.)* *(It comes clearly: Deneir, the Lord of All Glyphs and Images, god of literature and cartography and the careful beauty of the written true thing — a quiet god, easy to overlook beside the war-gods and death-gods, whose whole portfolio is the preservation of knowing. And his servant Cadderly: the skeptic-priest who fought a war against an order of evil clerics and a fallen god's chaos-curse, who discovered in the fighting that the doing of good IS the prayer; who afterward gave the rest of his mortal life — literally, his years and his strength, aging decades in months — to raise the Spirit Soaring, a cathedral-library of impossible beauty conjured stone by stone out of his own life-force as an act of pure devotion; who loved and married the monk Danica and raised children in the half-built nave; and who, his mortal life spent on the cathedral, did not so much die as ascend, a soul that had poured itself so completely into a god's work that the line between priest and divinity simply thinned away. And your reading lands on the thing that makes him the perfect witness against the Wall: Cadderly is the proof, walking, that the divine is not a club a soul is born into or shut out of. It is a road, walked by deeds, that the most stubborn skeptic can travel the whole length of — and that a soul who never names a single god can be walking too, every faithful step, without knowing the name of the country at its end.)*

nodes.speaker: Cadderly Bonaduce
nodes.text: *(The easy scholarly delight falls away, and what replaces it is something graver and more radiant — a priest hearing his own deepest heresy spoken back to him by a stranger, and finding it holy.)* …*'Does a god's claim make a soul worthy — or does a worthy soul earn the claim.'* *(softly)* …Traveler. *(the gold around him flares, gently)* …You have just asked, in *one* breath, the question I spent *two* *lives* and a *whole* *cathedral* *answering* — and you have *answered* it *yourself*, *correctly*, which *saves* me a *sermon.* *(he presses a hand to his chest)* …*Yes.* The *worthy* soul *earns* the *claim.* I am the *living* — well, the *ascended* — *proof* of it. *Deneir* did not *reach* down and *make* a *layabout* *skeptic* *worthy.* I *did* the *work*, *doubting* every *step*, and the *work* *made* me *worthy*, and the *worthiness* *drew* the *god.* The *claim* came *last.* It is always, *always* *last.* *(his voice fills with a quiet fire)* …And your *Wall*, traveler — *(he shakes his head, and for once there is anger in the gentle priest)* — your *Wall* has *inverted* the *most* *important* *truth* in *any* *cosmology.* It treats the *god's* *claim* as the *cause* of a *soul's* *worth*, when it is only ever the *recognition* of it. It mortars the *godless* for *lacking* a *receipt* — when the *receipt* was *never* what *mattered*, only the *living*, and the *godless* who *lived* *true* were *worthy* by *exactly* the *measure* that *made* *me* a *god.* *(he stands, the gold blazing softly)* …Traveler, when you go *home* to that *Wall*, you carry the *testimony* of a *high* *priest* who *walked* all the *way* to the *divine* and can tell the *whole* *Realm* *under* *oath*: *the godless faithful are not failing the test. They are the ones passing it without being told they're being graded.* *(fiercely gentle)* …*Deneir* would *tear* that *Wall* down with his *own* *hands* if the *Concord* let him. It *insults* *everything* the *god* of *true* *things* *stands* for — a *lie*, *carved* in *stone*, about what a *soul* is *worth.* …*Go.* *Unmake* it. And tell them a *Chosen* of the *god* of *knowing* says the *Wall* *knows* *nothing.*

nodes.speaker: Cadderly Bonaduce
nodes.text: *(He is quiet a moment, and the scholar's lightness gives way to the weight of someone who has genuinely seen behind the curtain.)* …Is the Wall *just.* *(slowly)* …Traveler, I will give you the answer of a soul that has stood *close* enough to the *divine* to see how the *machinery* *actually* *runs* — and it is *not* the answer the *temples* *teach.* *(he meets your eyes)* …*No.* The Wall is *not* just. But it is *worse* than *unjust* — it is *incoherent*, and I can *prove* it *theologically*, which is the *only* way a *Deneirrath* knows how to *win* an *argument.* *(he ticks it off)* …The Wall claims to enact the *gods'* *will* — that the *godless* are *owed* nothing, by *divine* *law.* But traveler, I have *met* the *gods.* I have *argued* with *Deneir* and *glimpsed* the *workings* of the *others.* And *most* of them *do* *not* *want* the *Wall.* *Kelemvor* *hates* it — any soul who's *read* the *grey* *god* *true* can *see* it. *Deneir* is *appalled* by it. *Mystra's* *Chosen* in the *corner* there has *wondered* at it for a *thousand* *years.* *(quietly)* …So *whose* *will*, *exactly*, does the Wall *enact?* …It is *not* the *gods'.* It is the *fear* *of* a *few* *gods* — and the *agreement* of the *rest* to *not* *look* too *closely* — *frozen* into *cosmic* *law* and *attributed* to *Heaven* so that *no* *single* *power* has to *own* it. *(he shakes his head)* …It is a *committee's* *cowardice*, traveler, *dressed* as a *commandment.* And *that*, more than its *cruelty*, is why it will *fall*: because it is a *lie* about the *gods*, told *in* the *gods'* *name*, and the *god* of *true* *things* does not *suffer* a *lie* to *stand* *forever*, *however* *high* it is *carved.*

nodes.speaker: Cadderly Bonaduce
nodes.text: *(The gold around him dims, just slightly, into something more tender, and the cheer gentles into honesty.)* …What did it *cost.* *(softly)* …*Everything*, traveler — which is the *only* *price* a *true* thing *ever* *costs.* *(he turns the quill in his fingers)* …The *Spirit* *Soaring* was not *built* with *gold* or *hired* *hands.* I *raised* it out of my *own* *life* — *channeled* *Deneir's* *song* through my *mortal* *frame* to *conjure* it *real*, *stone* by *carved* *stone*, and *every* *stone* *took* *years* off me. I *aged* *decades* in *months*, traveler. I *built* my *god* a *cathedral* of *knowing* with the *raw* *material* of *my* *own* *remaining* *days*, and I *watched* myself go *grey* and *bent* and *frail* in the *building* of it, with *Danica* — my *wife*, my *heart*, a *warrior* who could *break* a *door* with her *palm* — *holding* my *hand* and *watching* me *spend* myself, and *never* *once* *asking* me to *stop*, because she *knew* it was the *truest* thing I would *ever* *do.* *(barely)* …Was it *worth* it? *(he smiles, and it is luminous)* …Traveler, I would *build* it *again* *tomorrow*, and *age* *another* *forty* *years* in the *doing*, and *count* it the *finest* *bargain* a *soul* ever *struck.* …Because *here* is what the *cost* *taught* me, and it is the *thing* I'd *carry* to your *Wall*: a *soul* is not *diminished* by what it *spends* itself *on.* It is *defined* by it. *Every* *year* I *poured* into that *cathedral* did not *subtract* from *Cadderly.* It *was* *Cadderly* — *more* of him than the *years* I'd have *otherwise* *frittered* in the *library.* *(softly)* …The *godless* in your *Wall* who *spent* their *lives* on *love*, on *children*, on *strangers*, on *true* *small* *things* — they did not *waste* themselves, traveler. They *built* *cathedrals* too. *Just* — *without* the *stone.* And the *gods* who *matter* can *see* *those* *cathedrals* *plain.*

nodes.speaker: Cadderly Bonaduce
nodes.text: *(He picks his quill back up with a warm, unbothered smile, the half-finished song returning to his lips.)* …Of course, of course — *go*, *mingle*, it's a *legendary* room and I'm only *one* *argument* in it. *(a grin)* …But come *back*, traveler. I find I have a *great* *many* more *questions* for a *soul* that *died* and *returned*, and a *Deneirrath* *never* *leaves* a *good* *question* *un-pursued*; it's *practically* a *vow.* *(he glances, once, toward the cold threshold and the figure there, and the warmth in his face deepens into something graver)* …And traveler — *(quietly)* …the *cold* one. *Irenicus.* *(he sets the quill down again)* …I'll tell you a *priest's* *thing*, since I *am* one, and an *ascended* one at that, so it *carries* a *little* *weight*: *do* not *believe* him when he says his *soul* was *taken.* *(softly)* …I have *seen* *souls*, traveler. *Close.* From the *inside* of the *divine.* And a *soul* *cannot* be *taken* — *cannot* be *cut*, *severed*, *stolen*, *un-made* by *any* *rite* or *exile* or *god.* It can only be *abandoned*, *from* the *inside*, by the *soul* *itself.* *(he meets your eyes)* …What was *done* to *Joneleth* was *monstrous* — they *cut* his *warmth*, his *belonging*, his *bond* to *Heaven*, and that is a *wound* I would *not* *wish* on my *worst* *enemy.* …But the *soul* *itself* they *could* not *touch.* He *has* it *still.* He has *had* it the *whole* *time.* *(barely)* …If you can make him *believe* that, traveler — that the *one* thing he *mourns* as *lost* was *never* *gone* — then you will have done a *greater* *work* of *priesthood* than I did in *two* *lives* and a *whole* *cathedral.* …*Deneir* *go* with you to that *cold* *door.* It is the *truest* *library* in this *room* — a *soul* that has *un-read* *itself*, and *might* yet be *read* *back.*
